items.title: Add Cloudflare Tunnel for coordinator API access
items.problem_statement: The coordinator API runs on Railway with a provider-assigned domain. Cloud agents require a public HTTPS endpoint to reach it. Cloudflare Tunnel would allow running the coordinator locally while exposing it to cloud agents through a stable domain, eliminating Railway API hosting costs and reducing latency.
items.recommended_action: /plan-feature 'Add Cloudflare Tunnel and custom domain DNS for coordinator API'
items.why_now: Cloudflare domain already set up. Directly benefits gen-eval-testing (cloud agents need reliable coordinator access). Quick win with high infrastructure leverage.

items.title: Stable service URLs via Cloudflare DNS subdomains
items.problem_statement: Services use hardcoded localhost:808X URLs (30+ references) and provider-specific domains. Switching deployment targets requires updating COORDINATION_API_URL, SSRF allowlists, and agent configs everywhere. Custom subdomains (coord.domain.com, mcp.domain.com) decouple service identity from deployment location.
items.recommended_action: Combine with add-cloudflare-tunnel as a single change
items.why_now: Complementary to tunnel setup. SSRF allowlist (COORDINATION_ALLOWED_HOSTS) becomes a single wildcard instead of per-provider entries.

items.title: Implement generator-evaluator testing framework
items.problem_statement: The coordinator has 105+ interfaces (35 HTTP, 39 MCP, 31 CLI) with no cross-interface consistency testing, no adversarial multi-agent scenarios, no live MCP server testing, and no LLM-generated edge cases. A fully planned OpenSpec change (gen-eval-testing, 56 tasks) exists but has 0 tasks started.
items.why_now: Only active OpenSpec change with work remaining. Fully planned with validated proposal. Cloudflare tunnel would provide reliable cloud agent access for live service testing.

items.title: Zero Trust access layer via Cloudflare Access
items.problem_statement: Coordinator API protected only by X-API-Key headers. No IP allowlisting, mTLS, or identity-based access. Leaked API key gives full access. Cloudflare Access adds service tokens, IP restrictions, and audit logs without code changes.
items.recommended_action: /plan-feature 'Add Cloudflare Access Zero Trust layer for coordinator API authentication'
items.why_now: Builds on tunnel/DNS setup. Defense-in-depth for production coordinator. Service tokens are more rotatable than raw API keys.

items.title: Add CORS middleware and /ready endpoint to coordination API
items.problem_statement: Two beads tasks ready (act-58o, act-99c): CORS middleware for cross-origin API access and /ready endpoint for orchestration health checks. Custom domain makes CORS origin configuration predictable.
items.recommended_action: bd update act-58o --status=in_progress, then implement both tasks
items.why_now: CORS configuration benefits from known domain origin. /ready endpoint supports gen-eval-testing.

items.title: Webhook receiver on custom domain
items.problem_statement: Project uses ntfy.sh for webhooks and has GitHub webhook integration planned. A stable webhook endpoint (hooks.domain.com) with Cloudflare DDoS protection would support inbound notification channels and CI event receivers.
items.recommended_action: defer — implement after tunnel and DNS are in place
items.why_now: Natural extension of tunnel/DNS. Enables self-hosted webhook receiver replacing third-party dependency.

items.title: Multi-environment DNS isolation
items.problem_statement: No environment isolation for test vs production coordinator. Gen-eval-testing needs a test coordinator that doesn't interfere with production. Separate subdomains (coord-dev, coord-prod) with different backends.
items.recommended_action: defer — implement after single-environment DNS is proven
items.why_now: Needed before running gen-eval against production coordinator safely.

combined_recommendation: Combine add-cloudflare-tunnel + add-custom-domain-urls as a single change for maximum quick-win value
